source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior Backend Software Engineer on the DAO team, you will design, build, and maintain scalable systems that deliver a first-class experience for both Credit Union members and staff, ultimately driving growth in newly opened accounts.

You'll own problems end-to-end: from understanding business requirements and making architectural tradeoffs, to shipping reliable solutions with minimal oversight. This is a product that's still being shaped, you'll have real influence over how it evolves.

This role offers a unique opportunity to work closely with our Product and Design teams, as well as collaborate with external partners, giving you the chance to strengthen skills beyond engineering, such as communication, product thinking, and negotiation.




ABOUT THE TEAM

Credit Unions (CUs) strive to offer their members loans with competitive interest rates. To make this possible, the greater the members’ investment in the CUs, the more favorable the rates become. That’s why Digital Account Opening (DAO) plays such a critical role at Clutch, it enables customers to seamlessly open new accounts (e.g., Savings, Checking) and start investing their money, strengthening the overall financial ecosystem.

The team has skilled full stack engineers who collaborate closely to tackle challenging problems. We foster a supportive and hands-on environment where everyone contributes to designing and building secure, scalable solutions.

Our team thrives on collaboration, innovation, and a shared commitment to standardizing our solutions across all company products in a secure and efficient way.



What You’ll Do

Within 3 months, you will:

 - Be fully onboarded to your new team, familiarizing yourself with Clutch’s tools, processes, and culture.

 - Build strong relationships with your team members, leader, and cross-functional stakeholders.

 - Work in collaboration with developers and other stakeholders to enhance our platform.

 - Deliver your first feature to production within the DAO platform

 - Understand how CU-specific configurations work and how the team handles variation across clients

 - Be able to independently debug and troubleshoot issues within the DAO stack

Within 6 months, you will:

 - Ensure high-quality code, and enabling the efficient delivery of features and improvements.

 - Write elegant, efficient, and testable code that is easy to maintain and debug.

 - Take care of configurations and processes documentation so stakeholders understand how to use the platform

 - Own medium-to-large features from technical design through to production

 - Contribute to architectural discussions on how to scale CU-specific flows and configurations

 - Proactively identify and address technical debt or reliability gaps in your area

Within 9 months, you will:

 - As a true teammate, continuously improve the team’s processes, brainstorm ideas, and communicate statuses, and progress

 - Deliver a lot of new stuff to our products

 - Start identifying opportunities to deliver faster, reliable solutions and make initial contributions to architectural improvements.

 - Drive proposals for how to standardize and scale DAO configurations across CUs

 - Be a go-to person for your area of the codebase, unblocking teammates and reviewing their work

 - Contribute to cross-team discussions on platform-wide patterns and improvements



What You’ll Bring

 - 7+ years as a Software Engineer, with meaningful backend experience in fast-paced environments (SaaS and/or Fintech preferred)

 - Proficiency in backend technologies, preferably TypeScript, Node.js, and NestJS

 - Experience designing and building APIs and services in distributed systems; comfort making tradeoffs between speed, quality, and scalability

 - Strong debugging and problem-solving skills — you dig into root causes, not surface-level fixes

 - Familiarity with AI-assisted development workflows; you use AI tools to amplify your output while maintaining full accountabil

source_jobs.title: Senior Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Enterprise Customer Success Manager (CSM), you will own a portfolio of ~12–15 mid-market accounts, typically valued between $100K–$300K ARR each. These are complex, relationship-driven customers, all of them Credit Unions.

This role spans both executive onboarding during implementation (driving alignment, ensuring the customer is set up for long-term success) and post-implementation success (ongoing adoption, value realization, and growth).

The ideal candidate is comfortable moving between tactical execution (helping customers through technical challenges in tandem with our Implementation Engineers) and strategic partnership (advising executives on how to unlock value at scale).

This is a remote role open to US-based candidates with a small share of travel (approx. 15%)




ABOUT THE TEAM

The Customer Success team is a distributed group of 10+, led by two managers and a Director, supporting CSMs across the US. We work closely with executives at Credit Unions of all sizes, guiding them through onboarding, adoption, and long-term growth.

Our culture is high-trust and high-accountability. We value progress over perfection and empower CSMs to take ownership of their portfolio while collaborating closely with product, implementation, and support. The people who thrive here are resilient, bring positive energy, and act with a bias to action in an environment that is still evolving.

With the company on a strong growth path, this is a chance to join at a pivotal moment and help shape how we deliver value to customers while growing into larger, more strategic accounts.

What You’ll Do

Within 3 months, you will:

 - Take ownership of a portfolio of 12–15 accounts, including several large customers in active onboarding.

 - Build relationships with executive sponsors and key day-to-day contacts.

 - Lead the executive onboarding portion of implementations, ensuring stakeholders are aligned on goals and success measures.

 - Support adoption of core product capabilities and begin spotting areas for future expansion.

Within 6 months, you will:

 - Successfully complete onboarding for your in-flight customers and transition them into post-implementation success management.

 - Establish “3 wide, 3 deep” relationships across your accounts.

 - Partner with internal teams (product, support, implementation) to solve customer challenges and influence roadmap priorities.

 - Lead high-impact QBRs/EBRs (onsite & virtual) with executive stakeholders, supported by data-driven insights into customer performance and opportunities

 - Progress 4–5 qualified expansion opportunities, together with our Account Executives

 - Use customer data to surface actionable insights that drive adoption and executive alignment

Within 9 months, you will:

 - Be recognized by your customers as a trusted advisor who delivers measurable business value.

 - Consistently drive deeper adoption across your portfolio.

 - Work with the sales team to generate net-new opportunities through referrals

 - Generate at least 8 expansion opportunities across your book of business.

What You’ll Bring

 - 5+ years of experience as Customer Success Manager or other customer-facing role in a fast-paced environment, preferably in SaaS and/or Fintech or consulting

 - Willingness to travel up to 15 percent for QBRs, onboarding workshops, and executive meetings

 - Proven success managing a book of 10-20 highly complex B2B accounts, six figures in ARR each, where success depends on navigating fragmented decision-making, not managing high-volume transactional relationships

 - Executive presence and ability to guide senior stakeholders through onboarding and strategic reviews

 - Comfortable holding a point of view with senior stakeholders and pushing back constructively when the evidence supports it; doesn't confuse keeping the peace with doing right by the customer

 - Resilience and adaptability when working with fragmented or less mature customer organiz

source_jobs.title: Director of Professional Services
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

We’re looking for a leader to own and transform the delivery engine at Clutch, across Technical Delivery, PMO, and Solutions Consulting. As we scale, delivery is a core growth lever. How fast and efficiently we take customers live and into value directly sharpens our edge in the market.

At Clutch, delivery and product are deeply intertwined: how we configure and implement is itself part of the product experience. This leader works hand-in-hand with Product and Engineering, and is accountable for reimagining how delivery works end-to-end and building the operating model that enables it.

About the Team

Our Professional Services team is responsible for implementing Clutch products for our Credit Union customers, and carrying them to lasting value. We have a strong bench of managers and deep domain expertise. What we need is a leader who can point that talent at the right problems and get a fundamentally better motion out of the team, and holding both speed and quality at the same time.

What you’ll do

Within 1 month, you will:

 - Quickly build trust and credibility with your team and cross-functional partners, while forming a sharp POV on our delivery motion and where it’s leaving speed and efficiency on the table.

 - Identify and launch early high-impact changes, demonstrating a bias for action and experimentation rather than waiting for a full assessment cycle.

 - Own the delivery metrics that matter (CSAT, time to implementation, utilization, and delivery predictability), and establish the cadence and visibility to manage these metrics.

Within 3 months, you will:

 - Lead the team to operational changes that materially bring time-to-implementation down to our targets across all products, without trading away quality.

 - Reshape how the team is measured and incentivized to reward impact and outcomes over activity.

 - Raise the performance bar across the organization by building the systems that enable clear expectations, priorities, and feedback.

 - Reinvent roles on the team using scalable, innovative technology and methods to change how delivery work gets done.

Within 6 months, you will:

 - Institutionalize a scalable delivery operating model that holds as Clutch brings new products to market.

 - Develop the manager layer into leaders that autonomously own outcomes, drive performance, and grow their people.

 - Deliver clear, executive-level visibility into delivery health, risk, and economics of the motion, contributing actively to growth strategy & direction.

What You’ll Bring

 - 10+ years in SaaS Professional Services or Software Delivery, with 3+ years managing leaders, not just individual contributors.

 - A proven track record of scaling and transforming delivery motions end-to-end that lead to material efficiency gains in time & resourcing, without sacrificing quality or customer satisfaction.

 - Demonstrated success working hand-in-hand with Product and Engineering teams, being comfortable operating where delivery and product overlap.

 - Strong people leadership: ability to coach and develop managers, staff resilient teams, and drive alignment on vision and goals.

 - Bias for action and experimentation in an ambiguous, fast-paced environment, with the ability to ramp quickly.

 - High AI fluency and a drive to constantly reinvent how works get done, reaching for technology to reimagine “today’s role” rather than defaulting to traditional playbooks or how things have always been done.

 - Command in all directions: influencing executives, peer functions, and down through a leadership layer.

 - Strong understanding of SaaS implementation lifecycles, enterprise customers, and recurring-revenue economics.

 - Familiarity with PSA tools and SaaS delivery analytics.

 - Financial services, fintech, or other regulated-industry experience strongly preferred.

Please note that this role may evolve as our business needs change, so we appreciate your flexibility and adaptability.

What’s In It

source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

We are hiring a GTM Engineer to own the systems and data infrastructure that powers Clutch’s go-to-market motion. This is a technical ownership role — you’ll architect and maintain the foundation that Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success run on, and grow into building the AI-powered workflows on top of it.

The right person for this role is someone who gets the foundation right before reaching for automation. You care about clean data, reliable integrations, and systems that hold up. You get excited to architect new systems and layer AI into these systems to create outcomes.

About the Team

You'll be joining a small but driven RevOps team that moves fast and builds smart. We're here to make the revenue engine run seamlessly to grow revenue. Your work will be visible, impactful, and directly tied to how we grow. It's just the two of us, so we trust each other to take ownership and execute. You'll have real autonomy, a tight feedback loop, and a direct line to strategy from day one.

We work hard, we're building something from the ground up, and we care deeply about what Clutch is doing: turning credit unions into fintech lenders so 130 million Americans have access to a better, more responsible path out of debt.

What You'll Do

Within 3 months, you will:

 - Complete a full audit of the GTM stack and bring data quality from its current state to a reliable foundation

 - Build enrichment pipelines that keep account and contact data accurate and current

 - Deploy and configure all core integrations so tools are talking to each other cleanly

 - Identify website visitors, tweak lead scoring, and create automated routing so the right accounts surface to the right people without manual work

 - Launch the first fully automated campaign end to end — list builds, sequences fire, responses route, pipeline gets attributed

Within 6 months, you will:

 - Create a solution that ensures data quality stays reliable

 - Deploy outbound sequencing across the full AE team with personalized, data-driven messaging at scale

 - Deliver full marketing attribution so every dollar spent is connected to pipeline, including renewal and expansion

 - Have a custom intent signal engine surfacing buying signals the team can act on in real time across both new logo and customer accounts

Within 9 months, you will:

 - Run multi-channel orchestration where a single trigger fires coordinated actions across tools — including CS touch points with no human handoff required

 - Deploy renewal risk scoring that flags accounts showing early churn signals — before CS has to manually notice

 - Own a GTM infrastructure that operates with meaningful autonomy — and have a clear point of view on where it goes next

 - Build a customer health score that informs both Sales and Marketing, not just CS

What You'll Bring

 - 3–5 years in a RevOps, Sales Ops, or GTM Systems role

 - Hands-on Salesforce experience — you’ve built flows, fields, object relationships, and integrations, not just run reports

 - HubSpot experience, including managing the HubSpot ↔ Salesforce sync

 - Experience with AI tools and prompt-based workflow

 - Experience building enrichment pipelines (e.g., Clay) that pull external data into CRM records

 - Familiarity with outbound and sequencing tools like Apollo or Salesloft

 - Comfortable with ambiguity — you can scope a problem and propose a solution without waiting for step-by-step direction

 - High ownership mindset you catch your own mistakes, communicate proactively, and don’t go silent when things go sideways

 - B2B SaaS experience

 - Experience partnering with Finance, CS, and post-sale teams, not just Sales and Marketing

 - Fintech or financial services experience a plus

 - Familiarity with tools like Gong, RB2B, or similar — you don't need to know them all, but you pick things up fast

 - Comfortable writing lightweight scripts or automation logic (Python, JavaScript, or similar) to move and transform data betwee

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

Clutch is expanding its design team. We're looking for a Product Designer to craft intuitive, human-centered experiences that power our lending and loan-application workflows.

This isn't surface-level screen design. You'll work across the full service — the borrower applying, the credit union employee processing the application, and the legacy systems and compliance steps in between. The wins here come from designing end-to-end processes, not just polishing individual screens: simplifying genuinely complex workflows, clarifying high-stakes financial information, and making every step feel effortless and trustworthy.

You'll move fast, own your work, and know when to drive a decision yourself versus when to pull in your PM and engineering partners. And you won't live only in Figma: our designers use AI to prototype, explore, and design — and to ship code for simpler features themselves. AI is core to how this team works and what we build, and we want someone eager to push that further.

About the Team

At Clutch, design isn't just a department — it's the driving force behind our mission. We believe a seamless user experience can empower credit unions to compete and offer superior banking to everyday people. By crafting intuitive experiences, we help credit unions guide their members toward financial freedom.

Our growing design team is fully remote in the U.S. and Brazil. It is diverse, collaborative, ambitious, and empowered to take on our company's biggest challenges.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

Within 1 Month, you will:

 - Get oriented fast: Build a strong working understanding of Clutch's lending products, the borrower and operator journeys, and the compliance and legacy-system realities they sit inside, quickly enough to start contributing.

 - Build relationships and credibility: Establish yourself with the product managers, engineers, and credit union partners you'll work with, and start earning trust as a design leader on the team.

 - Get hands-on with AI: Ramp on Clutch's AI design tooling and practices, using AI to prototype and explore from week one.

Within 3 Months, you will:

 - Own a significant area end to end: Take a large, loosely-defined design project from discovery through delivery, defining the problem and scoping the work yourself.

 - Design & ship real flows: Lead design on a substantial piece of the loan-application experience, reducing friction for borrowers and operators alike.

 - Build, not just mock: Use AI to create working prototypes, and ship simpler production changes yourself in partnership with engineering.

 - Drive clarity through research: Run interviews, concept tests, and usability sessions to validate your decisions, and bring the insights back to your partners.

Within 6 Months, you will:

 - Think in systems: Map full workflows (handoffs, compliance touchpoints, edge cases) and design reusable, scalable patterns rather than one-off screens.

 - Own a critical, high-stakes workflow: Lead design end to end for a core part of the lending journey (e.g., intake or decisioning), including the ambiguity and cross-team dependencies that come with it.

 - Shape direction, not just execution: Bring research and data into roadmap and prioritization conversations, influencing what the team builds and why.

 - Raise the bar on team craft: Drive design critique, share what you're learning about AI-assisted workflows, and help improve process and documentation across the team.

 - Collaborate with judgment: Partner closely with PMs and engineers as a true thought partner, knowing when to run independently and when to bring partners in to turn user and business needs into production-ready designs.

What You’ll Bring

 - Experience: 6-8 years as a product designer, with a track record in fintech, SaaS, or other complex, workflow-heavy environments.

 - AI appetite and fluency: Genuinely excited about AI in design and already putting it to work, using AI to prototype, design, and ship simpler

source_jobs.title: Senior MLOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a Senior MLOps Engineer to be the data team's owner of production ML operations. You'll build the pipelines that take models from prototype to production, own the low-latency serving API behind our Next Best Action (NBA) engine, and stand up the monitoring, alerting, and reliability layer that keeps NBA models — and the LLM agents that consume them — healthy in production. This is a builder's role at a builder's moment: NBA is going live, the production ML platform is being shaped now, and you'll define how Clutch ships and operates AI for years to come. When there isn't active MLOps work, you'll also contribute to data engineering and machine learning work across the team.




ABOUT THE TEAM

The Data team today is five people: one data scientist, two data engineers, one data analyst, and one product manager. We're small, ambitious, and shipping fast — ML models heading to production, a serving API being built, and AI agents in active development. You'll be the senior MLOps voice inside the team and the operational bridge to HAL, the platform team that runs Clutch's agent runtime. Expect tight feedback loops, real autonomy, and a team that values pragmatism over purity.



What You'll Do

Within 3 months, you will:

 - Take ownership of the ML serving API that serves NBA recommendations, partnering with the data engineer who's been building it, and harden it for low-latency production traffic

 - Build the first repeatable deployment pipeline: model artifact → versioned, deployable, rollback-able production service, with infrastructure defined as code

 - Stand up the monitoring foundation: latency/error/drift dashboards, alerting, and audit/trace visibility across models and agents

 - Build a working relationship with HAL and become the data team's go-to on ML serving and reliability decisions

Within 6 months, you will:

 - Be the primary owner (with data engineer support) of the ML serving platform and deployment pipelines for NBA and our ML models

 - Have at least one production model and one production agent fully instrumented — versioning, monitoring, alerting, and multi-tenant gating in place

 - Define the data team's playbook for shipping a new ML model to production, end-to-end

 - Drive architectural decisions across APIs, processing pipelines, distributed compute, storage, search, observability, cloud infrastructure, and model-serving workflows

 - Mentor the data engineers on MLOps patterns so they can confidently support and extend the systems you own

Within 9 months, you will:

 - Operate as the technical lead within the data team for NBA production ML operations — the person other teams come to when they want to understand how Clutch ships and runs ML reliably

 - Have measurably improved cost and latency

 - Be shaping the data team's roadmap for the next generation of ML infrastructure, in partnership with the PM and data scientist

 - Help us decide what to hire next as the team scales

What You'll Bring

Required

 - 8+ years of experience in software, data, or ML engineering, with 4–5+ years running ML systems in production — you've taken models from prototype to production and own what happens after deploy

 - Strong Python — most of the work (serving API, pipelines, tooling, data pipelines) is in Python, and you're comfortable in production codebases, not just notebooks. Some TypeScript is involved for integration with our agent runtime — you don't need to be an expert, comfort with a second language is enough

 - CI/CD & deployment discipline. You build training and deploy pipelines that take a model artifact to a versioned, deployable, rollback-able production service, with automated testing and reproducible builds. You've implemented CI/CD for ML and built and maintained CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Bamboo, GitLab CI, or similar)

 - Infrastructure as code. You manage cloud infrastructure (AWS Lambda, ECS) with Terraform or equivalent — no click-ops, everything rev

source_jobs.title: Strategic Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

We are hiring a Strategic Account Executive to drive new business across the largest credit unions in the country. You will own the full sales cycle for Clutch's digital lending, account opening, and AI product suite, selling into complex, multi-stakeholder environments where the deals are bigger, the cycles are longer, and the impact is transformational. This is a high-autonomy, high-reward role for a seller who can navigate executive buying committees, build compelling business cases, and close six- and seven-figure platform deals.

About the Team

You will join a high-performing GTM organization that includes five Strategic AEs, a dedicated SDR, Sales Engineering, RevOps, and Marketing — all laser-focused on winning the credit union market. The team operates with a consultative, partnership-driven approach, working closely with strategic partners like TruStage to create joint value propositions. We are backed by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), moving fast, and building the category-defining platform for credit union digital transformation.

What You’ll Do

Within 3 months, you will:

 - Ramp on Clutch's full product suite (digital lending, account opening, AI), value propositions, and competitive positioning

 - Develop a deep understanding of how large credit unions buy, evaluate, and implement technology

 - Build your territory plan and begin outbound prospecting into your named accounts

 - Run your first discovery calls and product demos alongside Sales Engineering

Within 6 months, you will:

 - Own and progress multiple strategic opportunities through complex, multi-stakeholder sales cycles

 - Deliver tailored business cases and ROI models to VP and C-level decision-makers

 - Represent Clutch at industry events, conferences, and credit union league meetings

 - Collaborate with TruStage and other partners to create joint pipeline and co-sell into shared accounts

Within 9 months, you will:

 - Consistently build and maintain a pipeline of 3x+ your quota target

 - Close net-new strategic deals and forecast accurately against your quarterly targets

 - Serve as a trusted advisor to senior credit union executives, influencing their digital transformation roadmaps

 - Contribute to GTM strategy through market intelligence, competitive insights, and product feedback

What You’ll Bring

 - 7+ years of full-cycle B2B SaaS sales experience, with at least 3 years selling into enterprise or strategic accounts with deal sizes of $200K+

 - Proven track record of consistently meeting or exceeding quota in complex, consultative sales environments

 - Experience selling to senior executives (VP, SVP, C-suite) and navigating multi-stakeholder buying committees

 - Strong discovery, business case development, and negotiation skills — you sell on value, not price

 - Comfort with longer sales cycles (6–9 months) that require strategic account planning, multi-threading, and executive alignment

 - Experience in financial services, fintech, or credit union/banking technology is strongly preferred

 - Familiarity with MEDDPIC, Sandler, or similar enterprise sales methodologies

 - Excellent communication, presentation, and storytelling skills

 - High ownership mindset — you operate with urgency and accountability in a fast-paced, high-growth environment

 - Willingness to travel 35–45% for prospect meetings, industry events, and partner road trips

 - Proficient with AI tools: Claude, ChatGPT

Please note that this role may evolve as our business needs change, so we appreciate your flexibility and adaptability.

About Us

Clutch is a revolutionary vertical SaaS company, proudly backed by Andreessen Horowitz (A16z), aimed at revolutionizing the way Credit Unions engage and change the lives of their members. As a champion of financial well-being, we address the urgent need for affordable lending solutions in an era where the average American grapples with over $155,000 in household debt.  Unlike traditional financial insti

source_jobs.title: Director of Platform Engineering
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Director of Platform Engineering to own and scale the infrastructure, security, and IT operations that power Clutch's platform. Clutch is a vertical SaaS company (backed by A16z) that provides white-label lending and account opening software to credit unions across the US. Our platform serves 163 credit unions, processes sensitive financial data, and must meet strict compliance requirements (SOC 2 Type II, GLBA, CCPA).

This role is the single owner of our cloud infrastructure (AWS), security posture, compliance programs, IT operations, and vendor management. You'll report directly to the VP of Engineering and work closely with the CEO, COO, and Legal on strategy. This is a leadership role where you'll define the direction, build the team, and execute with high autonomy.




ABOUT THE TEAM

You'll lead a team spanning Infrastructure, Security, and IT. Today the team includes an Infrastructure engineering manager (Lucas Sousa) and his engineers, Security individual contributors, and a small IT support team. The broader engineering org is about 80 people across Brazil and the US, organized into five areas: Lending, Foundations, Security and IT, Account Opening, and cross-functional teams (HAL AI, Data). You'll work cross-functionally with every engineering team (they all depend on your platform), plus Finance (vendor budgets), Legal (compliance), and Customer Success (implementation environments). The culture is direct, accountable, and moves fast. We operate on a "You Build It, You Run It" principle and value completed staff work over escalation.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


WITHIN 3 MONTHS, YOU WILL:

 - Complete a full audit of infrastructure, security posture, vendor accounts, and IT operations. Identify gaps and quick wins

 - Take ownership of all vendor relationships and complete account transfers from the previous director

 - Establish your working relationship with every engineering team, Finance, Legal, and Customer Success

 - Assess and strengthen incident response protocols and cybersecurity monitoring (Datadog SIEM, Cloudflare)

 - Evaluate and stabilize IT operations, including support SLAs and the endpoint management program


WITHIN 6 MONTHS, YOU WILL:

 - Drive vendor rationalization. We have 30+ tools identified for review ($229K in immediate cuts, $1.2M under evaluation). Own the decision-making and execution

 - Implement improvements to our compliance program (SOC 2 Type II, GLBA) and own the next audit cycle

 - Optimize cloud infrastructure costs and performance across our AWS organization (multiple accounts, ECS Fargate, Aurora PostgreSQL)

 - Improve sandbox and environment management for customer implementations. Credit unions need secure, reliable sandbox environments for onboarding

 - Define and begin executing on the team structure and hiring plan for your org


WITHIN 9 MONTHS, YOU WILL:

 - Have a fully staffed and operating Platform Engineering org with clear ownership boundaries

 - Advance cloud security and data protection strategies, including improvements to per-partner data segregation and encryption (AES-256, FIPS 140-2 Level 3)

 - Lead a company-wide security awareness program and foster a security-first culture across engineering

 - Drive automation and tooling improvements that measurably reduce toil for the broader engineering org

 - Own and present the platform roadmap, including infrastructure scaling strategy for the next 12 months




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

Must-haves:

 - 8+ years of experience in platform engineering, infrastructure, and/or security leadership, preferably in B2B SaaS or fintech

 - Proven leadership experience managing infrastructure, security, and IT teams in a fast-paced environment

 - Deep knowledge of AWS (ECS Fargate, Aurora PostgreSQL, IAM, KMS, S3, VPC) and infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Terragrunt)

 - Strong understanding of security frameworks and compliance standards: SOC 2 Type II, GLBA, CCPA, and ideally PCI DSS

 -

notes: Highly technical roles, strong focus on engineering and product development
